message:
fix: send UiShutdown event to unblock async bridge after TUI exits

Add a shutdown_event_tx clone of the event channel sender and send
PiMsg::UiShutdown after the TUI run loop completes. This ensures the
async bridge task exits promptly even when background tasks still hold
event sender clones, preventing the process from hanging after the user
quits the interactive session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.5.2","title":"Add cancellation and checkpoint tests for long-lived loop changes","description":"Add tests that prove the loop-checkpoint changes materially improve cancellation and deadline behavior. These should be sharp regression tests tied to the specific loops changed by the cancellation workstream.","design":"Testing guidance:
- Bind each test to a loop or path that actually received a checkpoint change.
- Assert prompt cancellation observation or bounded deadline honoring.
- Avoid flaky timing assumptions.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when each changed looped path has behavior-focused cancellation or deadline coverage and the tests clearly encode the reason the checkpoint was added.","notes":"If the cancellation workstream narrows itself to a very small set of loops, this bead should narrow accordingly rather than manufacturing broad but weak test coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:34.578774150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T21:01:56.715142361Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T21:01:56.715124568Z","close_reason":"Implemented the approved checkpoint sites in src/tools.rs, src/rpc.rs, and src/agent.rs, documented the deliberate defers in docs/asupersync-leverage-inventory.md, and added focused cancellation/checkpoint regression tests for each changed loop.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","checkpoint","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:48.962898139Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:34.578774150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.5.3","title":"Add persistence and worker-lifecycle tests for approved blocking-work changes","description":"Add coverage for any approved worker-migration changes so runtime ownership, shutdown, and persistence semantics remain stable after the refactor. If the worker-evaluation workstream decides to keep all raw-thread islands, close this bead with explicit rationale instead of forcing artificial tests.","design":"Testing guidance:
- Cover only the worker migrations that actually land.
- Assert lifecycle and semantics that motivated the change: ownership, shutdown or quiescence, persistence correctness, or bounded behavior under cancellation.
- Do not create broad generic tests that are too weak to catch regressions.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when approved worker migrations have focused regression coverage or, if no migrations land, the bead is closed with an explicit no-op rationale.","notes":"This bead exists to validate meaningful worker changes rather than manufacture testing activity for its own sake.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:35.896184527Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:49.289529315Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","blocking-io","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:49.289474252Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:35.896184527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.6","title":"Evaluate supervision or AppSpec adoption only where lifecycle hazards justify it","description":"Evaluate whether any long-lived Pi surface should adopt a more explicitly supervised or AppSpec-oriented Asupersync topology. This is intentionally a question and spike rather than a committed refactor, because the review so far suggests this may be useful in a narrow area but not obviously enough to justify broad churn.","design":"Working rules:
- Start from concrete lifecycle hazards, not from library feature availability.
- Prefer a clear defer decision over a speculative architectural rewrite.
- Only prototype a supervised or AppSpec surface if the topology map exposes a real problem that the current structure handles poorly.","acceptance_criteria":"This workstream is complete when the topology and lifecycle analysis is explicit, a go or no-go decision is recorded, and either a narrow justified scope or an equally explicit defer rationale exists.","notes":"Likely candidate surfaces include interactive background orchestration, RPC lifecycle ownership, and compaction or background worker topology, but the burden of proof stays on demonstrating a concrete reliability or lifecycle win.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"question","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:37.178584468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:49.615372592Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","asupersync","asupersync-leverage","supervision"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:37.178584468Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:49.615278126Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4008,"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is a spike, not a mandate. A clean defer decision is a successful outcome if the current topology is bespoke but fundamentally sound. Only prototype supervision or AppSpec if the topology map exposes a concrete lifecycle hazard that current structure does not handle well.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:38Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-xdcrh.6.1","title":"Map long-lived service topology and concrete lifecycle hazards","description":"Map the long-lived service topology across interactive, RPC, and compaction-adjacent surfaces. This bead should identify who owns what today, how shutdown or quiescence currently works, and where the real lifecycle hazards are versus where the topology is merely bespoke.","design":"For each candidate surface, record:
- Entry and ownership boundary.
- Child or background tasks and workers involved.
- Shutdown and cancellation behavior today.
- Any real orphaning, quiescence, or restart or escalation hazard.
- Whether a supervised or AppSpec structure would materially simplify the situation.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when candidate long-lived surfaces have explicit topology and lifecycle notes, real hazards are distinguished from stylistic discomfort, and the result is specific enough to support a go or no-go decision.","notes":"This bead should piggyback on the inventory and blocking-worker evaluation results instead of rebuilding that work from scratch.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:38.798703730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:49.945138370Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","asupersync","asupersync-leverage","supervision"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:49.945074351Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:38.798703730Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-xdcrh.6.1","title":"Map long-lived service topology and concrete lifecycle hazards","description":"Map the long-lived service topology across interactive, RPC, and compaction-adjacent surfaces. This bead should identify who owns what today, how shutdown or quiescence currently works, and where the real lifecycle hazards are versus where the topology is merely bespoke.","design":"For each candidate surface, record:
- Entry and ownership boundary.
- Child or background tasks and workers involved.
- Shutdown and cancellation behavior today.
- Any real orphaning, quiescence, or restart or escalation hazard.
- Whether a supervised or AppSpec structure would materially simplify the situation.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when candidate long-lived surfaces have explicit topology and lifecycle notes, real hazards are distinguished from stylistic discomfort, and the result is specific enough to support a go or no-go decision.","notes":"This bead should piggyback on the inventory and blocking-worker evaluation results instead of rebuilding that work from scratch.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:38.798703730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:49:55.810280867Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","asupersync","asupersync-leverage","supervision"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:49.945074351Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:38.798703730Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.6.2","title":"Record adopt-or-defer decision for supervision or AppSpec, with a narrow prototype only if justified","description":"Record the final adopt-or-defer decision for supervision or AppSpec. A narrow prototype is allowed, but only if SUPER_MAP identifies a concrete lifecycle hazard that merits it. The deliverable is not a prototype for its own sake; the deliverable is a justified decision future contributors can trust.","design":"Decision guidance:
- If the current topology is bespoke but fundamentally sound, say so and defer.
- If one surface would clearly benefit from a supervised or AppSpec shape, define that surface precisely and keep the rest out of scope.
- Capture not just the answer but the reasoning and non-goals.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when it records a clear adopt or defer decision, any prototype remains narrow and tied to a real hazard, and future contributors can tell exactly why broad supervision adoption was or was not pursued.","notes":"This bead should explicitly resist the temptation to treat architectural novelty as success, because a high-quality defer decision is a valid and useful outcome.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:40.130077255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:50.614953960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","decision","supervision"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:50.614908535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:40.130077255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:50.270818974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.7","title":"Benchmark candidate changes and land only the blatantly accretive subset","description":"Benchmark and synthesize the outcome of this entire effort so Pi only lands the changes that are blatantly accretive. The point of the original review was not to maximize Asupersync usage as an end in itself; the point was to improve correctness, robustness, and potentially performance where the current architecture leaves easy wins on the table.","design":"The synthesis should explicitly compare before and after behavior on the dimensions that matter most here:
- Budget and deadline propagation through high-level control paths.
- Cancellation latency and shutdown or quiescence in changed loops.
- Ownership and lifecycle clarity in any migrated worker surfaces.
- Deterministic test strength and maintenance cost.
- Overall complexity delta.

Any candidate that is merely clever, invasive, or hard to justify empirically should be deferred even if it is technically possible.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when each candidate change is marked land or defer with rationale, landed changes have test coverage and clearly stated wins, deferred ideas are documented explicitly, and the root epic can be closed without anyone needing to consult an external markdown plan.","notes":"Use this bead as the final decision gate; the correct answer may be to land only a small subset if that subset is where the correctness and reliability ROI actually is.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:41.412978820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:51.919116190Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","benchmark","decision-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.7","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:41.412978820Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.7","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:50.940913384Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.7","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:51.271694833Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.7","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:51.598262240Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.7","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:51.919072017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4009,"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The original architectural conclusion was explicitly conservative: there is room to use Asupersync a bit better, but no obvious justification for a big rewrite. This final gate exists to preserve that discipline and make sure only the high-ROI subset actually ships.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xemyw","title":"PROPT-REGRESSION: session_index metadata roundtrip overflows u64->i64 boundary","description":"Detected by bd-26ecm validator run (p1_validation_20260214_203412). Failing test: session_index::tests::proptest_index_session_snapshot_roundtrip_metadata. Minimal failing input sets message_count=9223372036854775808 (i64::MAX + 1), left/right mismatch suggests truncation or cast boundary handling around src/session_index.rs:1567. Repro: rch exec -- cargo test --lib session_index::tests::proptest_index_session_snapshot_roundtrip_metadata -- --nocapture. Log: fuzz/reports/p1_11_20260214_203412.log","notes":"Reopened: regression reproduced again in current tree via rch exec -- cargo test --lib session_index::tests::proptest_index_session_snapshot_roundtrip_metadata -- --nocapture (failed with message_count overflow case).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurpleBridge","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:39:20.795797434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:13:01.876501722Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T03:13:01.876462269Z","close_reason":"Completed (reopened): re-applied overflow-safe SQLite conversion in src/session_index.rs via sqlite_i64_from_u64, added deterministic overflow regression test, and updated proptest to expect error for message_count > i64::MAX. Verified via rch targeted tests: session_index::tests::index_session_snapshot_rejects_message_count_over_i64_max and session_index::tests::proptest_index_session_snapshot_roundtrip_metadata pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","proptest","regression","session-index"]}
